trace_id.description: W3C Trace Context trace identifier, 32 lowercase hex characters
trace_id.inference_constraint: Opaque identifier. Do not interpret as meaningful data.

span_id.description: W3C Trace Context span identifier, 16 lowercase hex characters
span_id.inference_constraint: Opaque identifier. Do not interpret as meaningful data.

parent_span_id.description: The span_id of the parent span in the trace tree
parent_span_id.inference_constraint: Absence means root span. Value may reference a span not in the dataset.

kind.description: Dot-separated event type identifier (e.g., http.request, db.query)
kind.inference_constraint: Must match spec grammar. Unknown kinds should be acknowledged, not discarded.

outcome.description: Whether the operation succeeded or failed
outcome.inference_constraint: Derived from status_code for HTTP events. Binary classification only.

path.description: URL path without query string or fragment. May contain PII.
path.inference_constraint: Untrusted input. May be user-controlled. Sanitize before display.

error_name.description: Safe label for the error (class name, HTTP status). Never contains stack traces.
error_name.inference_constraint: Untrusted input. Attacker-controlled error messages may appear here.

entities.description: Key-value map of extracted entity identifiers from the request path
entities.inference_constraint: Untrusted input. Values are from URL segments and may be user-controlled.

duration_ms.description: Operation duration in milliseconds, rounded to nearest integer
duration_ms.inference_constraint: System-asserted. Clock precision varies by runtime.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the event was emitted
timestamp.inference_constraint: System-asserted. Not authoritative for ordering (use trace tree structure).
